commands.review_summary:
The branch lets node-mode startup skip onboarding when the active gateway registry record still has a bootstrap token and adds active/inactive bootstrap coverage in StartupSetupState tests.

Reproducibility: no. live high-confidence reproduction was run. Source inspection shows current master returns setup-required whenever node mode lacks a stored node token, before considering an active registry bootstrap token.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Human review should decide readiness after the draft has the required Windows validation and startup smoke proof; there is no narrow automated repair to make on this PR now.

target.title: fix(pairing): allow node bootstrap startup
target.body:
## Summary
- let node-mode startup proceed when the active gateway registry record still has a bootstrap setup-code token
- keep shared/operator-token records from bypassing node setup without a stored node token
- add startup setup tests for active vs inactive bootstrap gateway records

## Validation
- `git diff --check`
- not run locally: `./build.ps1` / `dotnet test` because this mac worktree has no `pwsh` or `dotnet`; draft PR CI is the validation surface

## Context
Crabbox alpha.12 install/signing verified, but fresh setup-code node bootstrap created a per-gateway identity file without exchanging/storing a node token. Startup was entering onboarding first because node mode ignored the registry bootstrap token, and onboarding could reset node mode before the connection manager connected.
target.author: vincentkoc
